info.name: Tourism API
info.description:
REST API for the tourism booking platform.

This collection is GENERATED. Do NOT edit `docs/postman/tourism-api.json` directly — edit the source files in `docs/postman/src/` and run `pnpm postman:build` instead. See `docs/en/runbooks/postman-auth.md` for the build workflow.

Folder source files live in `docs/postman/src/folders/` (one file per top-level folder). The build script merges them in the order listed by `folders` below.

exec: // Auto-refresh Supabase access token if expired or missing.
// Skipped for the Health folder (no auth required).
// Reset the generic Bearer slot up-front. If we fail to pick a valid
// per-role token below, the request goes out with an empty Bearer and
// the backend returns a clear 401 — instead of silently reusing a
// leftover token from a previous run (e.g. admin token leaking into
// fall back to customer credentials otherwise. Manual override via
// Per-role slots are deliberately distinct from the generic
// `accessToken` Bearer slot. Earlier versions used `accessToken` as
// BOTH the customer slot AND the generic Bearer slot, so any admin
// fetch would silently overwrite the customer's cached token.
console.warn('[pre-request] Supabase credentials missing for ' + (isAdmin ? 'admin' : 'customer') + ' — protected requests will fail with 401.');
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ email: email, password: password }) }
if (err) { console.error('[pre-request] Supabase auth failed', err); return; }
console.error('[pre-request] No access_token in response', body);

item.name: Health
item.description: Liveness + readiness probes. Public — no auth.

request.description: Returns 200 if the process is alive. Does NOT touch the database.
DB connectivity check. `data.checks.database` is `up` or `down`.

item.name: Auth
item.description:
First-time user sync from Supabase JWT. Both endpoints are idempotent — safe to call on every login.

The `Reset tokens` helper at the bottom is a test-only utility that clears all cached Supabase tokens from the environment, simulating a fresh logout. Use it before re-running the customer/admin sync flow to verify behaviour from a clean state.

request.description:
Upsert the JWT-bearing user as CUSTOMER. Body is optional — sub & email always come from the verified JWT.

**Auth:** Bearer Supabase JWT (auto-refreshed by collection pre-request).

Upsert the JWT-bearing user as ADMIN. Caller email MUST be on the `ADMIN_EMAILS` allowlist or the request returns 403.

**Auth:** Bearer Supabase JWT (uses adminEmail/adminPassword from environment).

response.name: 403 Forbidden — not on allowlist

exec: // Allow either 200 (promoted) or 403 (not on allowlist) — both are valid
// outcomes that the caller wants verified.

request.description:
Clears every cached Supabase token slot from the environment, then short-circuits before any HTTP call is made. Use this to simulate a logout between role switches (e.g. customer → admin → customer) so the next sync request fetches a fresh JWT instead of reusing a cached one.

No backend route is hit. The collection's pre-request script is bypassed via `pm.execution.skipRequest()`.

exec: console.log('[reset] All Supabase token slots cleared.');
// When `pm.execution.skipRequest()` runs there is no response to
// assert against. On older Postman runtimes that lack the API the
// request falls through to /health, which returns 200 — still a

item.name: Users
item.description: Profile of the JWT-bearing user. Both routes require `/auth/sync` to have been called at least once.

request.description:
Return the local DB profile for the JWT-bearing user.

**Auth:** Bearer Supabase JWT.

response.name: 401 — not synced

request.description: Partial update of the current user's profile. Only `fullName`, `phone`, and `locale` are mutable here. Email/role can only be changed by Supabase or admin tooling.

response.name: 400 — invalid payload

item.name: Destinations (Public)
item.description: Read-only catalog browsing. Public — no auth required.

request.description: Paginated list of ACTIVE destinations. Filters: `search` (name_en/name_vi, case-insensitive), `sortBy` (createdAt|updatedAt|nameEn|nameVi), `sortOrder` (asc|desc).
Public detail by slug. Returns 404 when slug is missing OR inactive.

item.name: Destinations (Admin)
item.description: Admin-only CRUD. Requires Bearer JWT of an ADMIN user (allowlisted email).

body.raw:
{
  "slug": "{{destinationSlug}}",
  "nameEn": "Hoi An",
  "nameVi": "Hội An",
  "country": "Vietnam",
  "region": "Central Vietnam",
  "descriptionEn": "Ancient port town in Central Vietnam.",
  "descriptionVi": "Phố cổ ven sông ở miền Trung Việt Nam.",
  "isActive": true
}

request.description: Creates a destination. 409 on duplicate slug.

exec: // Persist the new id so subsequent requests (Tours create)
// can reference this destination by UUID.

request.description: Admin list — sees inactive drafts. `isActive` filter is honoured here (it is force-overridden to true in the public endpoint).
Partial update. Renaming via `slug` works but breaks external bookmarks — use with care.
Hard delete. Returns 409 if any tour still references this destination.

item.name: Tours (Admin)
item.description:
Admin-only CRUD on the Tour entity. Public list/detail are added in Sprint B2.3, itinerary nested CRUD in B2.4. All requests require an ADMIN JWT (auto-selected by the collection pre-request script when path matches `/admin/*`).

The folder-level pre-request script ensures `{{destinationId}}` is populated by upserting a fresh destination — keeps the folder runnable on its own (e.g. via Newman) even if the Destinations folder cleanup ran first.

exec: // Always re-resolve destinationId via the seed-destination slug so
// stale ids (e.g. when the previous Destinations folder DELETE'd the
console.warn('[tours] cannot seed destination — baseUrl/adminToken missing');
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ slug: slug, nameEn: 'Tour Seed Dest', nameVi: 'Tour Seed Dest' }) }

body.raw:
{
  "slug": "{{tourSlug}}",
  "titleEn": "Hoi An Walking Tour",
  "titleVi": "Tour bộ Hội An",
  "summaryEn": "Half-day walking tour through the Ancient Town.",
  "summaryVi": "Tour bộ nửa ngày qua phố cổ.",
  "destinationId": "{{destinationId}}",
  "durationDays": 1,
  "maxGroupSize": 15,
  "basePrice": 49.5,
  "currency": "USD",
  "category": "DAY",
  "difficulty": "easy",
  "isPublished": false,
  "included": ["Local guide", "Bottled water"],
  "excluded": ["Personal expenses"]
}

request.description: Creates a tour. Returns 400 INVALID_DESTINATION when `destinationId` doesn't exist; 409 TOUR_SLUG_EXISTS on duplicate slug.
Admin detail. Returns the tour with `destination` joined. 404 when slug missing.
Partial update. Sending `destinationId` re-validates the FK; sending `slug` may break external bookmarks.
Hard delete. Returns 409 TOUR_HAS_BOOKINGS when bookings still reference the tour.

item.name: Tours (Public)
item.description: Public catalog browsing. No auth required. Both endpoints pin `isPublished: true` server-side, so drafts never appear here regardless of the caller.

request.description:
Paginated list of PUBLISHED tours. All filters are optional and AND-combined.

**Filters:**
- `destination` — destination slug (kebab-case)
- `category` — `DAY` | `PACKAGE` | `CUSTOM`
- `minPrice` / `maxPrice` — inclusive bounds on basePrice
- `duration` — exact day count
- `featured` — boolean
- `q` — free-text search (titleEn/titleVi/summaryEn/summaryVi)

**Sort whitelist:** `createdAt`, `basePrice`, `durationDays`, `titleEn`.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Convenience query for the marketing home page hero shelf. Returns the first 6 featured + published tours.
Filtering by a destination slug that does not exist returns an empty `data` array — NOT a 404. Catalog browsing returns the honest answer (no rows) rather than an error.
Public detail by slug, with destination joined for breadcrumbs. Returns 404 when the slug is missing OR unpublished — the two cases are deliberately conflated so draft slugs are not probeable.

item.name: Tours (Admin) — Itinerary
item.description:
Nested itinerary CRUD under `/admin/tours/:slug/itinerary`. Days are addressed by `(slug, dayNumber)` rather than UUID — humans (and Postman) read URLs like `/admin/tours/hoi-an-walking-tour/itinerary/7` more naturally than UUIDs.

**Pre-conditions** — this folder assumes:
1. The parent tour referenced by `{{tourSlug}}` already exists. Run `Tours (Admin) → POST /admin/tours` first if it does not.
2. `{{itineraryDayNumber}}` is set in the environment (defaults to `7`, deliberately above any real Day 1/2/3 to avoid colliding with existing itinerary entries).

The Newman cycle here is **Create → List → Update → Delete**, so the folder cleans up after itself and is safe to re-run.

exec: console.warn('[itinerary] tourSlug missing — run Tours (Admin) → POST /admin/tours first.');

body.raw:
{
  "dayNumber": {{itineraryDayNumber}},
  "titleEn": "Scratch Day",
  "titleVi": "Ngày test",
  "descriptionEn": "Created from Postman folder cycle.",
  "descriptionVi": "Tạo từ chu trình test Postman."
}

request.description: Creates one itinerary day. 404 `TOUR_NOT_FOUND` when slug missing; 409 `ITINERARY_DAY_EXISTS` when `(tourId, dayNumber)` already exists.
Lists every itinerary day for the tour, sorted ascending by `dayNumber`. 404 when the parent tour slug is missing.

exec: pm.test('returns an array of days', () => pm.expect(body.data).to.be.an('array'));

request.description: Partial update of one day. Sending `dayNumber` in the body renumbers it (subject to the unique constraint → 409 on collision).
Removes one day. Returns 200 + echo of the deleted row. 404 when the slug or day is missing.

item.name: Tours (Public) — Departures
item.description:
Public list of upcoming departures for one tour. No auth required.

**Defaults applied server-side:** `from = today` and `status = OPEN`. End users never see past or cancelled departures unless they explicitly pass the filters.

404 conflates 'tour slug missing' with 'tour unpublished' so draft slugs cannot be probed via 200-vs-404 diffing.

request.description: Returns the tour's upcoming OPEN departures, sorted ascending by `startDate`.

exec: pm.test('returns an array of departures', () => pm.expect(body.data).to.be.an('array'));
pm.test('all rows are OPEN by default', () => {

request.description: Demonstrates explicit filters overriding the public defaults. Status `OPEN | CLOSED | CANCELLED`.

exec: pm.test('all rows match requested status when present', () => {

item.name: Tours (Admin) — Departures
item.description:
Admin CRUD for departures, nested under `/admin/tours/:slug/departures`. The full Newman cycle is **Create → List → Update → Delete** so the folder is self-cleaning and re-runnable.

**Pre-conditions:**
1. Parent tour `{{tourSlug}}` exists (run `Tours (Admin) → POST /admin/tours` first if not).
2. The collection-level pre-request auto-uses the admin token because the path matches `/admin/*`.

The POST test script captures `{{departureId}}` into the environment so subsequent PATCH/DELETE addresses the same row.

request.description:
Creates one departure. 400 `INVALID_DATE_RANGE` when endDate < startDate; 404 `TOUR_NOT_FOUND` when slug missing.

`seatsBooked` is never accepted from clients — capacity counters are mutated only by the booking flow.

response.name: 400 INVALID_DATE_RANGE

exec: // Capture id for downstream PATCH/DELETE requests.

request.description: Lists every departure for the tour. No implicit `from = today` cutoff, no `status = OPEN` default — operators see CLOSED + CANCELLED rows for audit.

Partial update. Capacity guard: 400 `SEATS_TOTAL_BELOW_BOOKED` when shrinking `seatsTotal` below the row's `seatsBooked`. Sending only one of `startDate`/`endDate` re-validates the range against the unchanged side.
Hard delete. Refuses with 409 `DEPARTURE_HAS_BOOKINGS` when `seatsBooked > 0` — admins should mark CANCELLED via PATCH instead so booking history is preserved.

exec: // Clear so subsequent runs don't reuse a stale id.

item.name: Uploads (Admin)
item.description:
Mint short-lived signed upload URLs for Supabase Storage. The backend does NOT proxy file bytes — FE uploads directly to the signed URL.

**Pre-conditions:**
1. Supabase Storage bucket `tourism-assets` exists (see `docs/en/runbooks/uploads.md`).
2. Backend env has `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` set — signing requires the service role.

The response carries `{ uploadUrl, token, path, bucket }`. FE then calls `supabase.storage.from(bucket).uploadToSignedUrl(path, token, file)`.

request.description:
Returns 200 + envelope (no resource is persisted yet — this just reserves a path).

**purpose enum:** `TOUR_HERO | TOUR_GALLERY | DESTINATION_HERO | USER_AVATAR`. Each maps to a fixed folder under the bucket (see runbook for the layout).

response.name: 400 Bad Request (invalid filename)
502 STORAGE_SIGN_FAILED

request.description: Demonstrates a different `purpose` routing into a different folder (`destinations/hero/...`).

item.name: Bookings
item.description:
Customer-facing booking flow (Sprint B3.1–B3.3). The webhook surface for `checkout.session.completed` lands in B3.4 — for now, **created bookings stay PENDING after Stripe payment** because no webhook listener writes back yet.

**Pre-conditions:**
1. `tourSlug` points at a published tour (run `Tours (Admin) → POST /admin/tours` and set `isPublished=true`, or rely on the seed catalog).
2. The target tour has at least one OPEN departure with seats available. Easiest: run the Postman folder cycle in `Tours (Admin) — Departures` so `{{departureId}}` is set.
3. Caller is signed in as a customer (`/auth/sync` already ran). The folder uses the customer JWT slot automatically.

The POST captures `{{bookingCode}}` for the follow-up GET requests.

exec: // Sanity hints in the runner log when prerequisites are missing.
console.warn('[bookings] tourSlug missing — set it or run the Tours folder first.');
console.warn('[bookings] departureId missing — run the Departures folder to seed one.');

request.description:
Creates a PENDING booking and mints a Stripe Checkout session.

**Response:**
- 201 `{ data: { bookingId, bookingCode, checkoutUrl, status: 'PENDING' } }`
- 400 `DEPARTURE_NOT_OPEN` — departure is CLOSED/CANCELLED
- 401 `USER_NOT_SYNCED` — call `/auth/sync` first
- 404 `TOUR_NOT_FOUND` | `DEPARTURE_NOT_FOUND`
- 409 `SEATS_NOT_AVAILABLE` — best-effort capacity check failed

Open `checkoutUrl` in a browser to test the Stripe flow. Use test card `4242 4242 4242 4242` with any future expiry + any 3-digit CVC. The booking stays PENDING until B3.4 webhook lands.

exec: console.log('Open this in a browser to test Stripe:', body.data.checkoutUrl);

request.description: Lists the caller's bookings, newest first (max 50). Includes parent tour + departure summary so the FE can render the list without a follow-up join.
Owner-or-admin only. Non-owner customers see the same `404 BOOKING_NOT_FOUND` as a truly-missing code — we don't leak code existence to enumeration attacks.

exec: pm.test('returns booking with tour + departure joined', () => {
